title: Ensure disabling or scheduled deletion of customer created CMKs is monitored

input_row.title: Ensure disabling or scheduled deletion of customer created CMKs is monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for customer-created CMKs that have changed state to disabled or are scheduled for deletion.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Data encrypted with disabled or deleted keys will no longer be accessible. Changes in the state of a CMK should be monitored to ensure that the change is intentional.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> Internal Only - General ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{($.eventSource = kms.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName=DisableKey)||($.eventName=ScheduleKeyDeletion)) }" 4. Note the <disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for CMKs that have been disabled or scheduled for deletion and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: Internal Only - General aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{($.eventSource = kms.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName=DisableKey)||($.eventName=ScheduleKeyDeletion)) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-alarm> --metric-name <disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: Creation, storage, and management of CMK may require additional labor compared to the use of AWS-managed keys.
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79192-1 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Internal Only - General
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_kms_cmk_state_change_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
1. **AWS API Calls**:
   - Use AWS CLI to retrieve details about CloudTrail trails:
     ```
     aws cloudtrail describe-trails
     ```
   - Use AWS CLI to get the status of the trail:
     ```
     aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>
     ```
   - Use AWS CLI to check event selectors:
     ```
     aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>
     ```
   - Use AWS CLI to describe metric filters:
     ```
     aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>
     ```
   - Use AWS CLI to describe CloudWatch alarms:
     ```
     aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric>]'
     ```
   - Use AWS CLI to list SNS subscriptions:
     ```
     aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>
     ```

2. **Resources to Enumerate**:
   - Multi-region CloudTrail trails.

3. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**:
   - Verify that the CloudTrail trail is active and logging:
     - `IsLogging` should be `TRUE`.
   - Verify that management events are captured:
     - At least one event selector with `IncludeManagementEvents` set to `true` and `ReadWriteType` set to `All`.
   - Check that the metric filter for CMK state changes exists and matches the specified filter pattern.
   - Verify that an active CloudWatch alarm is linked to the metric.
   - Ensure that the SNS topic is setup and has at least one valid subscriber.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - **Pass**: 
     - Multi-region CloudTrail trail is active and logging.
     - At least one management event selector is configured.
     - Metric filter for KMS state changes exists and is correct.
     - CloudWatch alarm is active.
     - SNS topic has at least one subscriber.
   - **Fail**: 
     - Any of the above checks do not meet the conditions.

5. **Example Verification**:
   - Use the commands listed to check configurations and settings; aggregate results to determine if monitoring for the disabling or scheduled deletion of CMKs is properly set up.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE  
- The classification as Automated is correct. The checks can be performed using AWS CLI commands, which are suitable for automation. The use of AWS APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI is appropriate for this control, as it involves retrieving and verifying configurations and settings that can be automated.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8  
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The AWS CLI commands listed are valid and appropriate for the tasks described. However, there are some areas for improvement:
  - The `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors` command should ensure that the event selectors include management events with `ReadWriteType` set to `All`, which is correctly mentioned.
  - The `aws logs describe-metric-filters` command should specify the correct filter pattern for CMK state changes, which is implied but not explicitly detailed in the example.
  - The program name `aws_kms_cmk_state_change_monitoring_enabled` is somewhat descriptive but could be more specific to indicate that it checks for disabling or scheduled deletion of CMKs.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `aws_kms_cmk_state_change_monitoring_for_disable_or_deletion` to better reflect the specific checks being performed.
- Consider using AWS Config rules to continuously monitor the configuration of CloudTrail and CloudWatch resources, which could provide additional compliance assurance.
- Ensure that the metric filter pattern is explicitly defined in the documentation to avoid ambiguity.
- Verify that the SNS topic has not only a subscriber but also that the subscriber is confirmed, which is crucial for receiving notifications.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH  
- The assessment is well-structured, and the use of AWS CLI commands is appropriate for the automated checks required by this control. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the suggested improvements are minor.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify  
- Modify the program name to be more descriptive as suggested.
- Explicitly define the metric filter pattern in the documentation to ensure clarity.
- Consider adding a check for the confirmation status of SNS subscribers to ensure notifications are effectively delivered.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the control can be automated using AWS CLI commands and APIs, which are capable of retrieving and verifying the necessary configurations for monitoring CMK state changes. The tasks described, such as checking CloudTrail trails, event selectors, metric filters, and CloudWatch alarms, can all be performed programmatically using AWS APIs, making automation feasible and efficient.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_kms_cmk_state_change_monitoring_for_disable_or_deletion

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** AWS KMS, CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, CloudWatch Alarms
- **API/CLI Method:**
  - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
  - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
  - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<disable-or-delete-cmk-changes-metric>]`
  - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`
- **Properties to Check:**
  - CloudTrail trail is active and multi-region (`IsLogging` is `TRUE` and `IsMultiRegionTrail` is `TRUE`)
  - Event selectors include management events with `ReadWriteType` set to `All`
  - Existence of a metric filter for CMK state changes
  - Active CloudWatch alarm linked to the metric filter
- **Pass Criteria:**
  - All configurations are correctly set and active as per the control requirements
- **Fail Criteria:**
  - Missing or incorrect configurations, such as inactive trails, missing metric filters, or alarms not set up

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both agreed on the feasibility of automation, and the second opinion provided a more descriptive program name, which was adopted.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Amazon_Web_Services_Foundations_Benchmark_v4.0.1
